reason: Plan is already fully implemented and tested. All four acceptance criteria are satisfied with dedicated test coverage. The architecture follows the established signal-to-visual pipeline pattern (analyser → render loop → mappings → geometry system) without introducing new abstractions or cross-cutting concerns. Changes are scoped to five files in the existing module hierarchy with no new dependencies, no storage API violations, and no API surface changes.

modified_modules: src/visual/mappings.ts — added bassToMacro() and bassEnergy to VisualParams
src/visual/renderLoop.ts — added computeBassAvg(), EMA smoothing, synthetic fallback
src/visual/systems/particleField.ts — consumes bassEnergy as velocity multiplier
architectural_impact.dependency_graph_changes: None. particleField already consumed VisualParams; renderLoop already polled AnalyserPipeline. No new import edges.
architectural_impact.cross_cutting_concerns: None. Bass processing is contained within the existing per-frame data flow.
architectural_impact.public_api_changes: None. No new endpoints, exports, or user-facing contracts.

conformance.privacy_model: pass — no storage APIs, no raw identifiers, bass data stays in memory
conformance.audio_pipeline: pass — analysis continues while muted via AnalyserNode-before-GainNode routing; synthetic fallback covers silent/blocked states
conformance.accessibility: pass — prefers-reduced-motion scales motionAmplitude to 0.2, tested in T-018-05
conformance.performance: pass — buffer reuse via pre-allocated Uint8Array, no per-frame object creation, toroidal wrap avoids particle respawn cost
conformance.definition_of_done: pass — build succeeds, no console errors, no forbidden storage, works with/without autoplay, mute toggle and info overlay unaffected
